title: Smash the Stack
learning_objectives: Bypass modern exploit mitigations (ASLR, DEP, stack canaries)
scenario: Scenario Three custom vulnerable applications with stack-based buffer overflows. Develop working exploits for all three, from fuzzing through shell access. Subtasks Scoring Rubric Linked Recipes: RA04, RA05 ________________________________________________________________________________

scenario_context.setting: Gaborone, Botswana, Southern African mining conglomerate
scenario_context.challenge: Develop buffer overflow exploit against legacy application in Gaborone, Botswana
scenario_context.relevance: Reflects real-world African enterprise security challenges and red team operations across the continent

subtasks.title: Application 1: Fuzzing & Crash
subtasks.description: Fuzz the application. Confirm EIP control.
subtasks.evidence: Fuzzing script + crash evidence + EIP proof
subtasks.detailed_guidance:
Begin by thoroughly reviewing the scenario and understanding all objectives. Document your approach before execution. This is the foundational subtask that enables all following work.

Specific to this mission:
- Follow all engagement rules of engagement
- Maintain OPSEC throughout execution
- Collect evidence of all activities
- Document findings for client reporting
- Coordinate with engagement lead and blue team as needed
subtasks.evidence_required: Fuzzing script + crash evidence + EIP proof

subtasks.title: Application 1: Full Exploit
subtasks.description: Bad chars, JMP ESP, shellcode, working shell.
subtasks.detailed_guidance:
Conduct systematic reconnaissance and information gathering. Map the target network, identify services, and document all findings with evidence.

Specific to this mission:
- Follow all engagement rules of engagement
- Maintain OPSEC throughout execution
- Collect evidence of all activities
- Document findings for client reporting
- Coordinate with engagement lead and blue team as needed

subtasks.title: Application 2: Exploit Development
subtasks.detailed_guidance:
Analyze vulnerabilities discovered in reconnaissance. Validate each vulnerability through testing and document the exploitation path.

Specific to this mission:
- Follow all engagement rules of engagement

- Coordinate with engagement lead and blue team as needed

subtasks.description: Limited buffer space, more bad characters.
subtasks.detailed_guidance:
Execute the exploitation with careful attention to OPSEC and evasion. Maintain detailed logs of all actions for evidence and reporting.

Specific to this mission:

subtasks.description: How DEP, ASLR, stack canaries would prevent each.
subtasks.detailed_guidance:
Perform privilege escalation and lateral movement to achieve the mission objectives. Document each pivot point.

Specific to this mission:

- Coordinate with engagement lead and blue team as needed

quiz.question: Which mitigation technique makes buffer overflow exploitation most difficult?
options: Address Space Layout Randomization (ASLR) combined with stack canaries
quiz.explanation: ASLR randomizes memory addresses and stack canaries detect overwrites, making traditional buffer overflow exploitation very difficult.

quiz.question: In developing a buffer overflow exploit, what is Return-Oriented Programming (ROP)?
options: A programming language for network protocols
A technique to execute code using existing code segments to bypass DEP
A networking protocol for data transfer
A method for repairing broken code
quiz.explanation: ROP chaining existing code sequences (gadgets) enables code execution while bypassing Data Execution Prevention.

mentor_notes: For OA-M03 - Smash the Stack: Ensure learner demonstrates proper OPSEC, evidence collection, and professional reporting. This mission is designed to build advanced exploitation expertise. Coordinate with blue team for detection validation where applicable.
